q: How do I get pre-qualified for a mortgage loan?
a: Call your lender — or use our mortgage broker, who helps our clients get qualified. They should be able to get you a pre-qualification letter for a certain amount quickly, so you can go to showings of homes you are interested in and make an offer.

q: What is the typical timeline to buy a home?
a: Usually, from the time your offer is accepted to the time you close on your home takes between 30 to 45 days.

q: Why would I want to move to or live in the mountains of NC?
a: Breathtaking mountain views, cooler summers, affordability, and endless things to do — kayaking, canoeing, hiking, fishing, skiing, and shopping. Add friendly people, a safer environment, the Blue Ridge Parkway, four mild seasons, a lower cost of living, and a truly unique culture.

q: What kind of credit score do I need to buy a home?
a: Usually it needs to be a minimum of 620 or higher.

q: What should I consider when buying my new home?
a: Location, size of the home, price range per your pre-qualification letter, and the total size of the lot.

Earnest money is the amount the buyer puts down as an escrow deposit, showing the seller you are a serious buyer. This deposit is applied to the purchase price at closing, reducing the loan amount needed.

Due diligence money is a non-refundable fee paid by the buyer directly to the seller to secure an exclusive, negotiated period of time to inspect and evaluate the property. It compensates the seller for taking the home off the market while the buyer conducts investigations. At closing, this money is applied to the purchase price, reducing the loan amount needed.
